commit_scope: Close the keystone of the standing native-lowering gap: the int↔float BOUNDARY now lowers to native arm64 BYTES with no clang. The gap was stated as 'fd-f16 (with fpow, recursion) and the GQA block are not yet lowerable by form-lower (it does arithmetic trees, not recursion/float-recipes)' — on inspection that framing was partly stale (form-lower already lowers recursion via lo-rec-fn and float arithmetic via lo-fnode). What a real model block genuinely could not do is CROSS the lane: weights arrive as integer bit-fields (the f16 mantissa/exponent of f16-decode.fk, the q4k unpack) and rebuild as f64 (fd-frac's (mul 1.0 (fd-mant ...))), then cast back to int (round). form-asm.fk already byte-proved scvtf/fcvtzs against the clang oracle; the lowerer had no node to emit them. lo-i2f (tag 12) / lo-f2i (tag 13) wire them in. Adds the two lowering nodes, a four-way proof band (verdict 15), the fourth-arm manifest row, and the JIT_GAP_LEDGER #12 update.
evidence_refs: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/form-asm.fk form-stdlib/form-lower.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-lower-cvt-band.fk -> '✓ form-asm.fk+form-lower.fk+form-lower-cvt-band.fk → 15' and 'fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables)' and '1 ok, 0 divergent — kernels agree on every sample.'

clang/LLVM ORACLE (otool -tvVj of clang -c -arch arm64) confirmed every byte: scvtf d0,x0=9e620000, fcvtzs x0,d0=9e780000, fmov d1,d0=1e604001, fmul d0,d1,d1=1e610820, ret=d65f03c0. clang is NOT in the native path — the recipe lowers to bytes, clang only confirmed them.

Verdict bits: 1 to_f64(x)=float(x) lowers to the exact 8-byte image (scvtf reads x0 directly, no redundant mov); 2 round_trip(x)=trunc(float(x)) lowers byte-identical (the full int→f64→int crossing composes both ways); 4 sq_trunc(d)=trunc(d*d) lowers byte-identical (F2I/fcvtzs caps a register-form FP subtree fmul d0,d1,d1); 8 the round_trip image is exactly 3 instructions (12 bytes), one conversion each way, no junk.
Manifest row added: 'form-lower-cvt fkc 15' in form/fourth-arm-bands.txt, next to its kin form-lower-float.
No regression: form-lower (31), form-lower-float (15), form-lower-rec (31), form-lower-multiarg (127) all re-cross four-way after the change.

Companion gap observed NOT reproducing: 'fkwu broken on this local machine — verdict 0 for untouched f16-decode' did not reproduce on current main in this worktree. A freshly built fkwu computes the f16-decode band verdict 4095 four-way (validate: 'fourth arm: 1 band four-way'; standalone $FKWU $table 0 first line = 4095). The earlier local failure had healed — likely a stale binary from a prior emitter generation or the memory-pressure transient the kernel-memory-witness guards.

fourth_arm_outputs: form-lower-cvt: verdict 15, fourth arm 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables), 1 ok, 0 divergent — kernels agree

ci_validation.notes: CI has not run yet for this branch. The change adds two Form lowering nodes + a proof band + a fourth-arm manifest row; CI re-runs validate.sh across the suite.

deploy_validation.notes: Form stdlib lowerer recipe + proof band + ledger update; no public HTTP endpoint or service change. The recipe reaches the kernels through the repo, not a deploy.

phase_gate.reason: Local four-way validation passed (verdict 15, 0 divergent); CI and merge remain pending. No deploy applies to a Form stdlib lowerer recipe.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: form-lower can now lower a recipe that CROSSES the int↔float lane to native arm64 bytes with no clang: lo-i2f (scvtf, int→f64) and lo-f2i (fcvtzs, f64→int toward-zero) are byte-identical to the clang oracle and agree across Go/Rust/TS/fkwu at verdict 15. This is the keystone a real model block (f16-decode, q4k-dequant, the GQA block) needs to lower as ONE binary — it mixes integer field-extraction with float reconstruction.
public_endpoints: none changed; the recipe is a Form stdlib lowerer body proven by validate.sh
test_flows: Four-kernel proof: validate.sh runs form-lower-cvt-band on Go, Rust, TypeScript, and fkwu to the identical verdict 15 with 0 divergent.

Oracle byte-conviction: each lowered function image (to_f64, round_trip, sq_trunc) is byte-for-byte the clang -c sequence via fa-conviction; the round-trip trunc(float(x))=x is value-identity for integer inputs.
No regression: form-lower / form-lower-float / form-lower-rec / form-lower-multiarg re-cross four-way at their prior verdicts (31/15/31/127).
